overview.short: Tikona also known as Vitandgad, is the dominant hill fort in Maval in western India.
overview.description: Tikona also known as Vitandgad, is the dominant hill fort in Maval in western India. It is located near Kamshet around 60 km from Pune. The village nearest to the fort is called Tikona-Peth. The 3500 foot high hill is pyramidal in shape and the name Tikona means "triangular".

topPlaces.name: Gevhande Khadak
topPlaces.description: Gevhande Khadak is a village in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 285 ha (704 acres).

topPlaces.name: Thakursai
topPlaces.description: Thakursai is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in the Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 264.22 ha (653 acres).

topPlaces.name: Kothurne
topPlaces.description: Kothurne is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra, encompasses an area of 353.70 ha (874 acres).

topPlaces.name: Dhangavhan
topPlaces.description: Dhangavhan is a village in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra, India. It encompasses an area of 260.81 ha (644 acres).

topPlaces.name: Shivali
topPlaces.description: Shivali is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in the Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 504 ha (1,245 acres).

topPlaces.name: Yelghol
topPlaces.description: Yelghol is a village in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra, India. It encompasses an area of 477.94 ha (1,181 acres).

topPlaces.name: Pansoli
topPlaces.description: Pansoli is a village in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 65 ha (161 acres).

topPlaces.name: Pavana Lake
topPlaces.description: Pavana Lake, also known as Pavana Dam Reservoir and Pawna Lake, is a reservoir turned artificial lake in the Indian state of Maharashtra, formed by the Pavana Dam across the Pavana River in Pune district. The reservoir is 25 km from Lonavala and is increasingly getting popular as picnic and camping site for visitors from Pune and Mumbai.

Pavana Lake, also known as Pavana Dam Reservoir and Pawna Lake, is a reservoir turned artificial lake in the Indian state of Maharashtra, formed by the Pavana Dam across the Pavana River in Pune district. The reservoir is 25 km from Lonavala and is increasingly getting popular as picnic and camping site for visitors from Pune and Mumbai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tikona?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tikona is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (40 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tikona?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tikona at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tikona?
faq.a: Stays in Tikona start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tikona?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gevhande Khadak, Thakursai, Kothurne, Dhangavhan, Shivali.
